project.name: knifer-go
project.layout: public v* facade packages delegate to internal/* implementation packages

environment_diagnostics.notes: Reports local Go/tooling/Git state without modifying repository files.

worktree_check.notes: Fails when unrelated untracked Go files may pollute tests or commits. Use SKIP_WORKTREE_CHECK=1 only when intentionally excluding those files.

test.notes: Runs unit tests for PKGS, defaulting to ./....

quick_check.notes: Fast local gate; includes worktree-check, module verification, vet, architecture, tests, API snapshot, documentation checks, governance maturity, benchmark metadata, and whitespace diff checks.

agent_check.notes: Default AI/Agent-safe validation entrypoint; delegates to quick-check and detects security-sensitive diffs.

security_sensitive_diff.notes: Detects base, staged, unstaged, untracked, and deleted changed files under security-sensitive public facades and their internal implementations.

change_policy_check.notes: Detects base, staged, unstaged, untracked, and deleted changed file categories and maps them to change_type_policies.

aiflow_layout_check.notes: Verifies committed aiflow.yaml lives at the repository root and .aiflow/ remains ignored local runtime evidence/state.

agent_evidence.notes: Writes machine-readable Agent validation evidence outside the repository root, including base ref, deleted-file-aware diff metadata, detected policies, required commands, and command attestations.

agent_evidence_check.notes: Validates Agent evidence JSON schema, diff metadata, policy references, command references, command attestation coverage, risk calculation, and embedded check consistency.

agent_full_check.notes: Full AI/Agent validation entrypoint; runs race coverage, lint, and vulnerability checks.

agent_security_check.notes: AI/Agent security validation entrypoint; runs lint and govulncheck.

ci_test.notes: Local reproduction of the GitHub Actions test job without creating coverage artifacts in the repository root.

ci_agent_governance.notes: CI Agent governance gate; detects changed policies against AGENT_CHANGE_BASE_REF and emits validation evidence.

full_check.notes: Full local pre-push gate; includes race coverage, governance maturity, benchmark metadata, lint, and govulncheck. govulncheck may require vulnerability database access.

release_check.notes: Release readiness gate; runs full local checks plus CI workflow governance validation.

release_notes_check.notes: Validates CHANGELOG.md release-note structure and, when RELEASE_VERSION is set, verifies the tagged version has explicit release notes.

coverage_profile.notes: Generates a race/shuffle coverage profile outside the repository root.

coverage_check.notes: Requires a fresh coverage profile at the configured path and verifies security-sensitive packages have coverage data.

docs_check.notes: Checks generated documentation artifacts and facade quickstart cookbook structure.

docs_quickstart_check.notes: Validates that every public facade has a standardized docs/doc quickstart with required cookbook sections.

ai_context_check.notes: Validates ai-context.json schema, command side-effect metadata, facade inventory, and security-sensitive package references.

governance_maturity_check.notes: Validates API convergence, package lifecycle, error taxonomy, threat model, dependency isolation, and benchmark regression metadata.

bench_regression_check.notes: Validates benchmark regression metadata, tracked package coverage, and benchstat threshold declarations without running benchmarks.

ci_workflow_check.notes: Validates GitHub Actions workflow governance invariants declared in ai-context.json.

generate.notes: Runs repository go:generate directives and documentation generation targets. Only run after confirming generated output is expected.

docs_update.notes: Regenerates documentation artifacts that are derived from source code. Only run after confirming generated output is expected.

api_update.notes: Only run after confirming an intentional public API change.

tools_update.notes: Only run after confirming intentional facade, doc comment, or Example changes.

install_hooks.notes: Modifies local Git config core.hooksPath and file executable bits; do not run unless explicitly requested.

uninstall_hooks.notes: Modifies local Git config; do not run unless explicitly requested.

bench_core.notes: Runs benchmark baselines for core packages; output is evidence, not a performance claim.

bench_facade.notes: Runs benchmark baselines for facade packages; output is evidence, not a performance claim.

bench_codec.notes: Runs JSON/XML benchmark baselines; output is evidence, not a performance claim.

bench_smoke.notes: Runs the short benchmark health gate used by CI and release workflows. Output is evidence, not a performance claim.

fuzz_smoke.notes: Runs short fuzz/property smoke checks for packages with Fuzz targets. Increase FUZZTIME for deeper local exploration.

benchstat_compare.notes: Compares repeated benchmark outputs with benchstat. Requires benchmark files produced by separate runs and a local benchstat binary.

bench_baseline.notes: Writes repeated benchmark output outside the repository for historical baseline comparisons. Runtime can be long with BENCHCOUNT=10.

bench_compare.notes: Writes current repeated benchmark output and compares it against the saved baseline with benchstat.

api_freeze_check.notes: Validates v1 API freeze and deprecation governance metadata against the generated API catalog.

bug_fix.notes: Default policy for localized behavior fixes. Add coverage_profile and coverage_check when the fix is non-trivial.

public_api.notes: Public API additions or breaking changes must keep the API snapshot, tools catalog, and coverage evidence current.

internal_refactor.notes: Internal refactors should preserve behavior and coverage while passing architecture gates.

documentation.notes: Documentation-only changes should still pass metadata, API snapshot, tools catalog, and whitespace checks.

ci_governance.notes: CI and governance changes must keep Makefile targets and AI metadata synchronized.

security_sensitive.notes: Changes touching security-sensitive packages must include regression coverage and security validation.

dependency_change.notes: Dependency changes must verify module integrity and vulnerability exposure.

architecture_rules: Public v* packages are thin facades over internal/* implementations.
Public v* packages must not import other public v* packages.
Internal packages must not import public v* packages.
Every public v* package directory must contain doc.go with a package comment.
Production panic is allowed only for explicit MustXxx/PanicXxx APIs or documented compatibility cases.
Intentional public API changes must update docs/api/exports.txt with UPDATE_API=1 make api-check.
Intentional facade, doc comment, or Example changes must update docs/api/tools.json with make tools-gen.

api_catalog.recommended_profile_schema: recommended_entrypoints profiles: day-one, safe, error, options, compatibility; golden_path lists 1-7 APIs with use_when and avoid_when guidance

agent_import_rules: Import public v* facade packages from github.com/imajinyun/knifer-go; do not import internal/* from application code.
Choose one focused facade package per domain first, then combine packages only when the caller's workflow crosses domains.
Prefer Safe, E, and WithOptions variants when inputs cross trust boundaries, can fail, or need explicit provider/limit injection.
Use docs/api/tools.json as the machine-readable function index and docs/doc/README.md as the human quickstart map.
selection_rules: Slice transformation starts in vslice; map transformation starts in vmap.
Safe HTTP request or download workflows start in vhttp; use vresty only when Resty-style chaining is desired.
URL parsing, query building, normalization, and SSRF-aware resource checks start in vurl.
Security-oriented digests, HMAC, encryption, signing, PEM, and X.509 helpers start in vcrypto; non-cryptographic hashes start in vhash.
Struct/map property mapping starts in vbean; generic object-level convenience starts in vobj only after focused package boundaries are considered.
Configuration loading, profile overlays, environment expansion, and safe remote config start in vconf.
JSON object/path helpers start in vjson; XML tree and namespace helpers start in vxml.

top_entrypoints.intent: crypto, JWT, and secure random workflows
JSON, XML, file, and archive workflows
cache, IDs, logging, and application helpers

stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_stdlib_when: Prefer stdlib when a plain for loop or slices package call is shorter, allocation-free, and local to one call site.
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_go_knifer_when: Prefer knifer-go when the workflow needs reusable Map, Filter, GroupBy, Chunk, Page, or error-returning callback helpers.

stdlib_first_decisions.scenario: map lookup, clone, ordering, and transforms
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_stdlib_when: Prefer stdlib when direct map access, maps.Clone, maps.Copy, or slices.Sorted(maps.Keys(m)) expresses the whole operation.
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_go_knifer_when: Prefer knifer-go when the workflow needs Pick, Omit, Diff, MergeFunc, MapValues, FilterErr, GroupBy, or a documented non-nil map contract.

stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_stdlib_when: Prefer stdlib when strings, strconv, unicode, or regexp directly express the operation without project-specific semantics.
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_go_knifer_when: Prefer knifer-go when the workflow combines blank checks, case helpers, masking-friendly text cleanup, or reusable string predicates.

stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_stdlib_when: Prefer stdlib when the caller needs streaming, token-level control, Decoder options, or direct struct marshaling.
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_go_knifer_when: Prefer knifer-go when the workflow needs small in-memory object/array helpers, path lookup, formatting, or default-returning convenience around dynamic JSON.

stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_stdlib_when: Prefer stdlib when the URL and host are trusted and net/http gives clearer request, transport, and context control.
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_go_knifer_when: Prefer knifer-go when inputs cross trust boundaries and need SSRF-aware URL validation, allowed-host policies, bounded reads, or safe download helpers.

stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_stdlib_when: Prefer stdlib when the caller is composing primitives directly and needs full crypto/rand, crypto/cipher, crypto/x509, or crypto/tls control.
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_go_knifer_when: Prefer knifer-go when the workflow needs reviewed helper combinations for HMAC, AES-GCM, RSA-OAEP/PSS, parameter signing, JWT, or secure token bytes.

stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_stdlib_when: Prefer stdlib when encoding/json, flag, os.LookupEnv, or direct assignment keeps the data shape explicit and small.
stdlib_first_decisions.prefer_go_knifer_when: Prefer knifer-go when the workflow needs tag-aware Copy, DecodeResult metadata, StrictUnused, DecodeHook, profile overlays, environment expansion, or safe remote config loading.

api_decision_card_template: Problem: what user workflow or compatibility gap requires a public API change?
Package boundary: which v* facade owns the behavior, and why not a neighboring package?
Proposed API: names, signatures, option types, error behavior, and Recommended/Compatibility/Experimental/Deprecated status.
Alternatives considered: standard library, existing knifer-go helper, internal-only helper, or external package.
Safety and errors: trust-boundary handling, context/cancellation needs, panic policy, and errors.Is/errors.As contract.
Examples and docs: godoc, Example functions, quickstart matrix, docs/api/tools.json, and ai-context metadata updates.
Validation: focused tests, fuzz/property checks when applicable, API snapshot update, tools generation, and benchmark/benchstat evidence for hot paths.
metadata_refresh_triggers: Run make tools-gen after intentional facade, doc comment, or Example changes.
Run make ai-context-check after ai-context.json, ai-context.schema.json, Makefile, workflow, facade inventory, security domain, or coverage gate changes.
Run make docs-quickstart-check after docs/doc quickstart or public_facades changes.
Run UPDATE_API=1 make api-check only after confirming an intentional public API change.

notes: Core facades should avoid third-party runtime dependencies unless explicitly allowlisted by bin/check_arch.sh.
Heavy extension facades isolate optional integrations such as Sentry, Logrus, Excelize, Resty, and barcode/image adapters.
Provider contract facades expose interfaces and adapters while keeping concrete clients, credentials, dictionaries, or NLP engines outside core.

data_transform.purpose: Dynamic data conversion, object shaping, structured serialization, and configuration binding.

collections.purpose: Typed collection helpers for slices, maps, and sets.

text_parsing.purpose: String processing, regular expressions, matching, tokenization, and language text adapters.

trust_boundary.purpose: Helpers that cross URL, HTTP, filesystem, archive, CLI, and configuration trust boundaries.

security_primitives.purpose: Cryptography, token, random, identifier, masking, and password safety primitives.

runtime_adapters.purpose: Runtime integration helpers, provider-backed adapters, external protocols, and heavier optional integrations.

domain_helpers.purpose: Focused small-domain helpers with narrow behavior and low dependency expectations.

required_questions: When should I use vjson instead of encoding/json?
When should I use vfile instead of os, io, or fs?
How should I handle untrusted JSON input?
How should I handle untrusted file paths?
When should I choose bounded reads or explicit errors?

non_goals: No OAuth or OIDC provider implementation

required_contracts: raw passwords are not logged or stored
non_goals: No account lifecycle or reset flow

scope: EC and OKP explicitly deferred unless implemented with fixtures
required_contracts: RSA public JWK parse and export
non_goals: No JWKS cache or refresh loop
No token validation policy inside vcrypto

required_boundaries: production secret material uses secure random or key management
deterministic readers are test-only injection points
private keys and encoded hashes are treated as secrets
SM2 UID policy is explicit at call sites
RSA OAEP and PSS options are interoperability choices
PEM and JWK exchange are key material helpers
benchmark paths must be bounded for CI smoke
password hashing benchmarks require explicit opt-in evidence
benchmark output is evidence not a universal performance claim
stdlib first for short local code
knifer-go for safety defaults and governance gates
standard library first for local loops
daily utilities should stay beside safety-focused facades
benchmark output is local baseline evidence
performance claims require repeated runs and benchstat
quick gates are deterministic and bounded
long or workload-specific benchmarks are manual opt-in
do not publish universal performance claims
Safe context-aware or WithOptions flows for trust boundaries

weak_facade_example_density_governance.selection_rule: common facades with example coverage below 25 percent
weak_facade_example_density_governance.ratchet_policy: raise selected weak facades in small increments instead of completing every API at once

weak_facade_example_density_governance_2.selection_rule: common facades with example coverage below 25 percent
weak_facade_example_density_governance_2.ratchet_policy: raise selected weak facades in small increments instead of completing every API at once

required_boundaries: standard library first for local loops
do not copy every helper from lo or lancet

taxonomy.use_when: Caller-provided arguments, payloads, or configuration values are malformed.
Requested key, file, field, record, or resource does not exist.
Input type, operation, algorithm, extension, or provider capability is unsupported.
URL, path, archive member, redirect, or remote resource fails a safety policy.
Operation exceeds an explicit time budget or context deadline.
Injected provider, filesystem, transport, codec, random source, or adapter fails after valid input.
Unexpected implementation failure that does not fit a caller-actionable category.

checklist: Public API snapshot is intentional and current in docs/api/exports.txt.
docs/api/tools.json and docs/api/tools.md expose Recommended API profiles for every public facade.
ai-context.json validates command metadata, dependency tiers, security domains, coverage gates, and v1 checklist ownership.
Quickstart docs cover helper choice, related packages, benchmarks/trade-offs, FAQ, conversion/decode/copy semantics, and dependency tiers.
Security-sensitive packages meet coverage gates and use Safe/E/WithOptions variants at trust boundaries.
Benchmark claims are backed by repeated benchmark runs and benchstat output.
Fuzz/property smoke checks pass for packages with fuzz targets.
blocking_exit_criteria: No stale generated artifacts or public API snapshots.
No architecture-rule violations or heavy dependency bleed-through.
No unresolved lint, govulncheck, coverage, fuzz smoke, or release-note readiness failures.
No undocumented public API additions or unclassified recommended entrypoints.

decision_cards.decision: Public facade discovery is frozen around at most five primary golden-path APIs per package.
decision_cards.rationale: A smaller entry budget keeps the library API-selectable and prevents agents from treating every helper as an equally preferred starting point.
validation: docs/api/tools.json golden_path entries match ai-context.json api_convergence.primary

decision_cards.decision: Dynamic Decode/Copy/Merge/JSON/object/config semantics are frozen as executable contract tests and fuzz targets.
decision_cards.rationale: Dynamic helpers are high-risk because callers depend on edge-case behavior for nils, unused fields, conversions, map replacement, and round trips.

decision_cards.decision: Heavy optional dependencies remain isolated to their owning facade/internal package families.
decision_cards.rationale: Core utilities must stay dependency-controlled so importing simple helpers does not pull Sentry, Logrus, Excelize, Resty, or barcode/image stacks.

decision_cards.decision: Cross-package errors converge on the seven GK_* codes and errors.Is/errors.As-compatible wrapping.
decision_cards.rationale: A stable error taxonomy makes failures diagnosable across facades without forcing callers to parse package-specific strings.

decision_cards.decision: Security-sensitive facades require STRIDE/DREAD threat coverage tied to misuse and contract tests.
decision_cards.rationale: Trust-boundary helpers must remain security-auditable through explicit tests for SSRF, traversal, weak randomness, SQL/argument injection, and transfer limits.

freeze_checks: Every intentional public API addition or signature change must have an API decision card.
Every deprecated API must include a replacement and rationale before v1 freeze.
docs/api/exports.txt snapshot must stay current through api_check.
docs/api/tools.json tools catalog must classify every API status and expose golden_path guidance.
Experimental APIs are not allowed while v1_candidate is true.

threat_model.methodology: STRIDE with DREAD severity scoring at each trust boundary; every security-sensitive facade must keep misuse tests for its highest-risk input paths.
